g.text: A computer can independently check the grounding of 92 of 99 objections in v3

text.style: fill: #444444; font: 9.5px 'TeX Gyre Pagella', 'Palatino', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start

g.text: All 99 v3 seeded-defect concerns, by how their grounding is resolved. v3 persists every briefing, so the in-briefing majority is now

text.style: fill: #444444; font: 9.5px 'TeX Gyre Pagella', 'Palatino', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start

g.text: machine-checkable - closing the v2 corpus's ~7%-only ceiling, which was a data-loss artifact, not a tooling gap. §8 / §10.2.

text.style: fill: #8a2f2f; font: italic 8.4px 'TeX Gyre Pagella', 'Palatino', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start

g.text: Existence is model-free; correct-application is cross-vendor (Claude+OpenAI). The residual limit is now LEGITIMACY (is a concern real) - not coverage - which

text.style: fill: #8a2f2f; font: italic 8.4px 'TeX Gyre Pagella', 'Palatino', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start

g.text: routes to the domain-expert audit, not the verifier. Directional, synthetic; n=99 concerns / 12 cases. Source: v3-run/coverage.json (derived from case-file codings).
